reasoning: The baseline price on 2025-11-09 (using Friday, Nov 7 close) is 86.70. For a stock, short-term moves are largely a random walk (~0.50), but the YFinance statistical analysis indicates a negative linear trend over the last quarter (falling from ~$95 down to the current $86.7), yielding a linear probability of 0.46 for the stock to be higher in one week. The combined tool estimate of 0.39 is artificially pulled down by the "seasonal" check which checks if the stock was >$86.7 last year (it wasn't, because it traded in the $70s). Thus, the linear model of 0.46 is a strictly better anchor. Despite better-than-expected Q3 earnings on October 21 (15% YoY revenue growth, non-GAAP EPS beat padding 56% operating margins), the share price dropped post-earnings before recovering to 86.70 over the last few days. Following the instructions to anchor on the statistical trend but stay close to 0.5: for the 1-week horizon (Nov 16), I predict 0.44 due to the near-term quarterly downward momentum. For the 1-month horizon (Dec 9), I predict 0.48 as the downward trend regresses toward the mean and the positive Q3 fundamentals take hold. For the 3-month horizon (Feb 2026), equity risk premiums and broad market drift dominate over current momentum, pushing the probability slightly above even to 0.51.
